title: Magnet Interaction Quiz
instructions_for_student: For each pair of poles approaching each other, predict whether they will attract or repel. Try to get all four correct!

additionalProps.title: Magnet Interaction Quiz
additionalProps.subtitle: Predict whether the magnets will attract or repel

scenarios.explanation: N–N are like poles, so they repel each other.
N–S are unlike poles, so they attract each other.
S–S are like poles, so they repel each other.
S–N are unlike poles, so they attract each other.

_tool_meta.subject: Science
_tool_meta.activity_reference: Activity 4.5: Let us experiment — Attraction and Repulsion between Magnets
_tool_meta.icons: inline SVG only — no lucide-react or external icon libraries imported

agent_readme.description: A sequential 4-scenario prediction quiz. Each scenario card shows two colour-coded bar magnets (red=N, blue=S) facing each other with their active poles labelled. Student clicks Attract or Repel. Correct answer locks green; wrong locks red and shows what the correct answer was. After all scenarios a score screen appears with per-round breakdown, star rating, and the golden rule summary. The magnets animate apart (repel) or together (attract) after answering.

subtitle.default: Predict whether the magnets will attract or repel

scenarios.description: Array of quiz scenarios. Each scenario shows two magnets approaching each other.

leftPole.description: The active pole of the LEFT magnet (facing right)

rightPole.description: The active pole of the RIGHT magnet (facing left)

correctAnswer.description: Physics: unlike poles → attract, like poles → repel

explanation.description: One-line explanation shown after student answers

customisation_examples._comment: 2-round quick quiz — only the two repulsion cases

additionalProps.title: Repulsion Check
additionalProps.subtitle: Which pole pairs push each other away?

scenarios.explanation: N–N like poles repel each other.
S–S like poles repel each other.

customisation_examples._comment: 6-round extended quiz with repeated scenarios for extra practice

scenarios.explanation: N–N are like poles — they repel.
N–S are unlike poles — they attract.
S–S are like poles — they repel.
S–N are unlike poles — they attract.
Unlike poles always attract — well done!

decision_tree.default_4_scenarios: Covers all 4 pole combinations once — N-N, N-S, S-S, S-N
decision_tree.fewer_scenarios: Reduce to any 1-3 scenarios from the array
decision_tree.extra_practice: Duplicate or add scenarios for more repetition
decision_tree.change_explanations: Edit the 'explanation' field for curriculum-specific wording
